carbon_policy_signal.item: EU Commission weakens methane enforcement rules under US pressure; energy-security exemptions drafted for gas operators.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Regulatory relief lowers operator abatement cost and marginal gas supply cost; reduces TTF floor and caps upside on EUA from tightened scope.

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale as of 2025-12-26 (136 days old) and Clean Dark as of 2025-12-31 (131 days old); rankings and trends from these metrics should not be treated as current intraday signals.
risk_flags: Storage at 35% (12.4pp below 5-yr seasonal) with refill pace critical to H2 power curve stability.
Libya gas ramp capacity-constrained; Iran war creates direct TTF supply gap with weeks-horizon tightness.
EU methane rule weakening may unlock marginal gas supply but signals policy volatility around energy-security carve-outs.
Coal at 96 USD/t (8th pctile) structurally uncompetitive vs. renewables (49% of load) and carbon; thermal dispatch compressed.

base.summary: Gas supply tightens on Iran disruption and Libya ramp constraints; storage refill steady; renewables 50% load.

downside.summary: Libya gas ramp accelerates; Russia methane exemptions unlock supply; mild weather and high renewables soften demand.

upside.summary: Libya pipeline outage or Iran Hormuz escalation; storage refill stalls; thermal call extends sharply into summer.

themes: Geopolitical gas squeeze: Iran disruption + Libya capacity constraints + Russia sanctions
Storage deficit driving seasonal refill urgency and thermal call extension
Coal historically cheap (8th pctile) but structurally sidelined by renewables and policy acceleration
top_drivers: Iran war + Libya supply ramp constraints tighten nearterm gas availability; TTF weeks-horizon risk, Cal+1 power squeeze.
EU storage 12pp below seasonal average (35% full) drives urgency on refill pace; thermal generation call extended into summer.
Russia shadow-fleet sanctions reduce Urals/LNG export optionality; supports TTF and raises NW Europe LNG arb premium.
Coal at 8th pctile (96 USD/t) structurally sidelined; renewables at 49% of load and policy acceleration (Portugal phase-out) compress thermal dispatch.
top_takeaway: Iran war tightens gas supply via Libya pivot and shadow-fleet sanctions; EU storage 12pp below seasonal average amplifies near-term Cal+1 power risk.

watchlist.why: Supply substitution mechanism for Iran war disruption; pipeline integrity and production ramp timeline drive TTF curve May–Jun.
Energy-security carve-out may release marginal Russian/non-OPEC gas; regulatory relief lowers abatement cost and TTF floor.

watchlist.metric: Storage refill pace (35% full, 12pp below seasonal)
watchlist.why: Thermal generation margin and Cal+1 power spread shape; slow refill extends summer demand signal and coal-to-gas arb breakeven.
Middle East oil/LNG export route security; indirect TTF and Brent-to-power fuel-switch elasticity via LNG arb premium.
Signal of EU-wide fast-track renewable acceleration post-Iran shock; bearish long-term power structural and merit-order displacement.

watchlist_dated.name: EU methane rule exemptions finalization
watchlist_dated.why: Energy-security carve-out completion drives gas supply and TTF floor signal
